architecture.summary: The paper introduces an attribution framework to trace hallucination origins using internal signals of LLMs, combined with a Differential Penalty Decoding (DPD) strategy that adjusts post-probabilities during decoding to reduce hallucinations. The system analyzes internal LLM states to diagnose hallucination categories and applies penalty adjustments to output tokens based on reliability signals.

biblio.title: Attributive Reasoning for Hallucination Diagnosis of Large Language Models
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: We introduce an attribution framework to trace the origins of hallucinations based on the internal signals of LLMs
We develop a new benchmark named RelQA-Cate, which includes eight categories of hallucinations for the answers generated by LLMs
We present a novel Differential Penalty Decoding (DPD) strategy for reducing hallucinations through adjusting post-probabilities of each answer
The performance on answer reliability has significant improvement, achieving 28.25% at most
